---
title: "Module 2 Assignment"
author: "Your Name Here"
format: 
  html:
    embed-resources: true
    self-contained: true
    math: mathjax
editor: visual
---

## Introduction

This assignment will be working with the [gapminder.csv](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/jmayberr/math133-stat-learning-fall2026/refs/heads/main/Datasets/gapminder.csv) which contains information on gdp per capita, life expectancy, and population for 142 different countries every five years starting in 1952.

Each problem will be graded on a 2-point scale with

2 = solution is fully correct with proper coding and explanations

1= solution is at least half correct with mostly proper coding and explanations

0 = solution is less than half correct

If you submit the assignment before the due date (not the accept until date), I will provide feedback and give you a chance to resubmit.

## Problem 1

Load and filter the dataset to include only the measurements for 2007 and all continents except Oceania. Fit a regression model `lifeExp ~ continent + gdpPercap + pop` and show the summary of results.  

Answer the following questions about your model in question 1: 

- Which predictors were significant?
- Interpret the coefficients of continent. What was the baseline and what do the other coefficients say about lifeExp in the other continents?  


## Problem 2

Create a scatterplot of lifeExp vs gdpPercap with points and trendlines colored by continent (and please change the eye-sore natural colors). Explain why this graph suggests evidence of an interaction between gdpPercap and continent (hint: are the slopes the same?) and then test to see if the interaction is significant for any of the continents by fitting the model `lifeExp ~ continent*gdpPercap`. 


## Problem 3

Create a log-log graph of _lifeExp_ vs _gdpPercap_ colored by continent. Then fit a model of the form `log(lifeExp)~ log(gdpPercap)*continent` and discuss the significance of the coefficients. Compare the $R^2$ with the model from Problem 2. 

## Problem 4

The $R^2$ values from `summary()` tell us how well each model fits the training data, but as we discussed in Module 1, a more honest evaluation is to test predictive performance on new observations the model has never seen. Compute the predicted values and test RMSE for each of the following models on the 2002 gapminder data (again filtered to exclude Oceania): 
- `lifeExp ~ continent + gdpPercap + population` (Problem 1)
- `lifeExp ~ continent*gdpPercap` (Problem 2)
- `log(lifeExp)~ log(gdpPercap)` (Problem 3 without continent) 

Hint: For the third model, remember that your predictions are on the log scale so you will need to back-transform them by taking `exp()` before computing RMSE.

Answer the following:

- Which model has the lowest test RMSE?
- Does the ranking of models by test RMSE agree with the ranking by training $R^2$?
- Why might a model with higher training $R^2$ perform worse on new data?


## Problem 5

Pick the model from 4 which you think fits the data best and create a residual plot for your this model. Comment on any patterns you observe. Identify any outliers by country and discuss their potential impact on the model fit.
